reasoning: The NFL and ESPN announced a non-binding agreement on August 5, 2025, under which the NFL would acquire a 10% equity stake in ESPN in exchange for NFL Network, RedZone, and other assets. However, multiple legal and media sources indicate that transitioning this to a definitive binding agreement, securing NFL owner approvals, and traversing the required DOJ antitrust and regulatory review will take 'roughly a year'. News outlets explicitly state the deal is on course to be finalized during the 2026 season. Since 'taking a stake' involves the transfer of equity at closing—and finalizing a massive media merger within just 4.5 months (August to December 2025) is practically impossible due to antitrust wait periods—it is highly unlikely the conditions will be met by the end of 2025.
